content: Just as the linguistic meaning of "reflexion" encompasses both physical light reflection and deliberate metacognitive反思, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be reframed as a dual-process system that first "bounces" reasoning off existing stored knowledge (the physical reflection parallel) to generate inconsistent perspective outputs, then engages deliberate metacognitive processing to refine responses. This dual framing unlocks a new structured design for LLM self-reflection that splits the mechanism into two distinct sequential stages aligned with the word's dual core definitions.
